title: Multipart Commands, Part Ledgers, and Cleanup · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent TypeScript team receives different tenants, browsers, file sizes, media, regions, compliance, traffic, and cost constraints and transfers multipart commands, part ledgers, and cleanup into a new typed multipart state machine. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of typed multipart state machine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, persist upload ID, part number, offset, size, checksum, ETag, attempt, and owner so completion uses the exact accepted part list.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, bound part workers and bytes, cancel safely, drain results, preserve completed evidence, and prevent async task or buffer leaks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Multipart Threshold Part Size
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 2872 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate readonly multipart part evidence and AbortSignal-driven cleanup; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Multipart Threshold Part Size.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Multipart Threshold Part Size, keep Multipart Threshold Part Size connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Abort Lifecycle Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-abort-lifecycle-cleanup-1l5evy" that uses case 9387 to validate readonly multipart part evidence and AbortSignal-driven cleanup. Independent transfer: choose the fixture, defend the invariant, and document a counterexample without scaffold. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Abort Lifecycle Cleanup.
